name: Samsara Fleet API
what_it_does: IoT and fleet management platform API providing real-time vehicle tracking, driver behavior monitoring, trip history, safety event detection, and sensor data from connected physical assets.
use_cases: Building fleet management dashboards with real-time vehicle location and status
Automating dispatch and route optimization based on live GPS data
Monitoring driver safety scores and triggering coaching workflows
Integrating telematics data into ERP or logistics platforms
Alerting on vehicle fault codes, harsh braking, or speeding events
not_for: Consumer vehicle tracking — requires Samsara hardware installed in each vehicle
Software-only fleets — every asset must have a physical Samsara device
Small operators without budget for hardware + SaaS subscription
best_when: An agent needs to orchestrate or monitor a fleet of vehicles or assets that already have Samsara hardware installed, especially for logistics, safety, or compliance automation.
avoid_when: Your fleet doesn't have Samsara hardware, or you only need basic GPS without telematics depth.

auth.notes: API token generated in the Samsara dashboard. Bearer token authentication on all requests. Tokens can be scoped to specific API categories (vehicles, drivers, etc.) within the dashboard UI.

pricing.paid_tiers: Enterprise SaaS subscription bundled with hardware; pricing not publicly listed
pricing.notes: API access is included with Samsara hardware + software subscription. No self-serve API access — requires sales engagement and hardware deployment.

performance.rate_limits: 100 requests/second per token; some streaming endpoints have separate limits

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Well-structured JSON error responses with descriptive messages and error codes. Documentation covers common error scenarios with examples.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Read operations and data queries are fully idempotent. Mutation endpoints (e.g., driver assignments) lack explicit idempotency key support.
known_agent_gotchas: Hardware must be physically installed — no simulated data without real devices
Real-time location data can have 30-60 second delay depending on device connectivity
Some endpoints have data lag during cellular dead zones; agents must handle stale timestamps
Webhooks require public HTTPS endpoint — agents behind NAT need tunneling or proxy
API version changes announced in advance but breaking changes do occur

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced on all endpoints. Bearer token auth with dashboard-configured scope categories. SOC2 Type II certified. GDPR compliant for EU fleet data. FMCSA compliance for ELD data handling.
